one_line_thesis: American Airlines is the largest U.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
American Airlines is the largest U.S. network airline by several traffic measures, operating a consolidated hub system with a material loyalty franchise. FY2025 results show revenue stability near $54.6 billion but margin pressure: operating income fell sharply versus FY2024 while debt remained near $29 billion. The business is understandable as a capital-cycle airline, but the bear case is not tightly bounded at the equity layer given leverage, and we see no durable moat.

At about $15.14 per share, we expect 28.48% per year over seven years on the synthesis base case (Lawrence owner-cash ...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Entry prices were computed mechanically from the routed power-zone profile (Predictable cash-flow security). They are decision inputs, not a decision; the owner must review the scenarios before acting.
pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: Per-share claims use fully diluted shares from valuation.inputs.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of AAL, including mid-cycle network operations, AAdvantage co-brand optionality, net financial claims, and cycle/leverage reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating network cash is valued once; cash, debt, co-brand option, and cycle reserve are separate additive components with unique overlap keys.

economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q filing extracts; GAAP net income is cross-check only for levered airline.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Co-brand and partner mile contracts renew over 3 to 7 years; base case assumes continued monetization through FY2030.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Judgment from industry loyalty precedents; American does not disclose standalone AAdvantage segment economics in SEC filings.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Partner-funded mile sales require no incremental owner capital in base case.

low.label: FY2025 net cash from operating activities
Q1 2026 diluted shares (net income / diluted EPS)
Normalized owner cash per diluted share (OCF proxy)
low.rationale: FY2025 operating cash flow per share anchors mid-cycle network cash generation; capital spending exceeds GAAP free cash flow but OCF reflects ticket and loyalty economics.

low.label: Duration-adjusted owner-cash capitalization multiple
low.rationale: Bear stresses fare wars and higher fuel; base mid-cycle seven-year path; bull modest network recovery without peak-cycle heroics.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Power Zones select the fitting economic method and eligible independent reviewers; they never choose the valuation answer or capital decision.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

personas.falsifier: FCF yield < 0.5% or commoditized archetype (turnaround/optionality only)
personas.label: Chris Hohn / TCI

personas.falsifier: dhando -> none OR net-debt/EBITDA > 3 OR bear return < -15%
personas.label: Mohnish Pabrai

timeline.label: Q1 2026 10-Q: revenue $13.9B, net income $382M; diluted EPS $0.58; total debt $27.7B
FY2025 10-K: revenue $54.6B, operating income $1.5B (down from $2.6B prior year); OCF $3.1B; capex $3.8B
FY2024 10-K baseline: revenue $54.2B, operating income $2.6B as post-pandemic normalization peak
8-K: debt refinancing and balance-sheet management actions during industry deleveraging cycle
Post-merger US network integration largely complete; focus shifts to debt reduction and fleet renewal
424B5 equity issuance during pandemic liquidity raise (historical capital structure event)
424B5 follow-on equity raise as part of pandemic survival financing
Warren Buffett / Berkshire SC 13G on American Airlines stake (later exited 2020)
Berkshire Hathaway SC 13G/A amendment on airline holdings
US Airways merger closes; American becomes largest U.S. airline by some traffic metrics

industry.structure: U.S. airline industry is an oligopoly among American, Delta, United, and Southwest with high fixed costs, union labor, and route-network scale advantages; pricing power is cyclical and contested by ultra-low-cost carriers on marginal routes.
industry.share_shift: Legacy carriers gained share post-pandemic as capacity disciplined; American leads in some domestic metrics but competes intensely with Delta and United on premium and corporate accounts.
industry.trend: Fleet renewal, loyalty program monetization, and debt paydown dominate capital allocation; fuel and labor costs remain the primary margin swing factors in 2025–2026.
